projects.latheacq.overview.title: site.hopen
Overview
site.hclose
projects.latheacq.overview.1: I am not prone to illness. I have not been sick in the past seventeen years and was not disposed towards taking sick days off of
work. I found myself needing the proverbial 'mental health day' on one occasion after being diddled out of some overtime and becoming
thoroughly disenchanted with the whole affair. As serendipity would have it, whilst being on the prowl for useful discarded materials
on my unplanned 'mental health day', I happened across what appeared to be part of a lathe. It was in an advanced state of decrepitude
and appeared to be missing both the tool-rest and tailstock. The latter, however, was eventually discovered amongst assorted junk nearby.
projects.latheacq.overview.2: I had my wife stand guard over the machine whilst I negotiated a price and paid for it. I then carried it several hundred metres
back to the car, only to discover later that it weighed just over 85kg without the tailstock attached. I guess I was pretty keen.
projects.latheacq.overview.3: At the time, it was unclear whether the machine would be restored or simply cannibalised for parts and scrap. I'd already been attempting
to build a metal lathe from first principles, but progress was slow and difficult. Perhaps this machine could be restored to working
condition — or perhaps it could serve as the foundation for building something else.
projects.latheacq.overview.4: Once I examined it properly, however, it seemed a shame simply to scrap it. The old machine was a mess, but I was up for the challenge.
